subject: Physics
title: The Multiverse Hypothesis
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Multiverse Hypothesis.

hook.text: Our universe might be one of countless universes, each with different physical laws. This is the multiverse idea.

buildup.text: Eternal inflation suggests that our Big Bang was just one bubble in an infinite ocean of expanding space.

discovery.text: Each bubble universe could have different constants—different strengths of forces, different particle masses.

twist.text: String theory predicts 10^500 possible universes. If all exist, everything that can happen does happen somewhere.

climax.text: The multiverse can't be observed or tested directly. Critics call it philosophy, not physics.

punchline.text: Infinite universes. Every possibility realized. Beautiful theory. Untestable.

quiz.question: Why is the multiverse hypothesis controversial?
options: It cannot be directly observed or tested
It requires faster-than-light travel to confirm
